question_title: Will Microsoft list at least one exploited Windows vulnerability in a Patch Tuesday release before May 1, 2026?
summary: This question asks whether Microsoft will list at least one exploited Windows CVE in a Patch Tuesday release dated between Feb 24 and May 1, 2026. The February Patch Tuesday (Feb 10) had 6 exploited Windows zero-days but falls outside the window. The March Patch Tuesday (March 10) is within the window but had NO exploited Windows CVEs — only publicly disclosed, non-exploited zero-days. The April Patch Tuesday (April 14) hasn't happened yet and is the primary remaining opportunity. Historical base rate is ~83% (5/6 recent months had exploited Windows CVEs). Combined with possibility of revisions and OOB patches, estimate P(YES) ≈ 90%.

factors.description: Extremely high historical base rate: 5 out of 6 recent months (Oct 2025-Feb 2026) had exploited Windows CVEs in Patch Tuesday releases.
April 2026 Patch Tuesday (April 14) is within the resolution window and hasn't happened yet. Based on base rate (~83%), very likely to include at least one exploited Windows CVE.
March 2026 Patch Tuesday (March 10) is within the window but had NO exploited Windows CVEs. One opportunity already passed without YES.
Microsoft sometimes revises CVE exploitability status after initial release. Six March CVEs were rated 'Exploitation More Likely', any could be upgraded to 'Exploited: Yes'.
Out-of-band patches provide additional opportunities beyond regular Patch Tuesdays for exploited CVEs to be disclosed.
The threat landscape in 2025-2026 shows accelerating exploitation. Microsoft patched 1,129+ vulnerabilities in 2025 alone with exploited zero-days nearly every month.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.90 (90% probability YES)**

**Key reasoning:**

- **Base rate is extremely high**: 5 of the last 6 Patch Tuesdays (Oct 2025–Feb 2026) included at least one exploited Windows CVE. This is the normal state for Microsoft's security ecosystem.
- **March 2026 already missed**: The March Patch Tuesday (March 10) is within the resolution window but had NO exploited Windows CVEs — only publicly disclosed, non-exploited zero-days. This consumes one of the two main opportunities.
- **April 2026 is the key remaining chance**: The April 14 Patch Tuesday is well within the window. Given the ~83% monthly base rate, it's very likely to include exploited Windows CVEs.
- **Additional pathways to YES**: Microsoft can revise CVE exploitability status after initial release (6 March CVEs were rated "Exploitation More Likely"), and out-of-band patches can also include exploited CVEs.
- **P(NO) requires a rare two-month streak**: Both March AND April would need to lack exploited Windows CVEs, AND no revisions/OOB patches would add one. This is historically uncommon given the active threat landscape.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will Microsoft list at least one exploited Windows vulnerability in a Patch Tuesday release before May 1, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42639)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if, in any Microsoft Security Update Guide release note dated on or after February 24, 2026 and before May 1, 2026, Microsoft lists at least one CVE affecting Windows for which the “Exploited” status is “Yes.” This question resolves to no if no such Windows CVE is listed with “Exploited: Yes” in that period. The primary source is the Microsoft Security Update Guide release notes at [https://msrc.microsoft.com/update-guide](https://msrc.microsoft.com/update-guide)
fine_print: Count only CVEs that Microsoft indicates are exploited (“Exploited: Yes”), and only if Windows is an affected product in the guide. If the MSRC interface changes, resolve using credible sources per [https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) Candidate Sources: Microsoft Security Update Guide main portal (backup) [https://msrc.microsoft.com/update-guide](https://msrc.microsoft.com/update-guide) | CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog (backup cross-check only) [https://www.cisa.gov/known-exploited-vulnerabilities-catalog](https://www.cisa.gov/known-exploited-vulnerabilities-catalog)
